articles.title: Heatwave: 5 homemade drinks to stay hydrated during intense summer - Odisha TV

articles.full_text:
As temperatures soar during peak summer months, staying hydrated becomes not just important, but essential for survival and overall well-being. Excessive heat can lead to dehydration, fatigue, heatstroke, and a drop in energy levels. While packaged drinks may seem convenient, they often contain added sugars and preservatives that do more harm than good.
Why Hydration Matters in Extreme Heat:
During intense summer, the body loses water rapidly through sweat, leading to an imbalance of essential electrolytes. This can cause dizziness, headaches, and even serious health complications if ignored. Experts suggest that along with drinking enough water, consuming natural, nutrient-rich fluids can help replenish lost minerals, maintain body temperature, and boost immunity. Homemade drinks, prepared with simple kitchen ingredients, are not only cost-effective but also far healthier alternatives.
5 Homemade Drinks To Keep You Hydrated
1. Lemon Water (Nimbu Pani)
A classic summer cooler, lemon water is rich in vitamin C and helps restore electrolyte balance. Add a pinch of salt and sugar for an instant energy boost.
2. Buttermilk (Chaas)
Made from curd, buttermilk aids digestion and cools the body. Adding roasted cumin powder and a pinch of salt enhances both taste and benefits.
3. Coconut Water
Naturally packed with electrolytes like potassium and magnesium, coconut water is one of the best drinks to prevent dehydration and heat exhaustion.
4. Aam Panna (Raw Mango Drink)
Prepared from boiled raw mangoes, this tangy drink helps prevent heatstroke and keeps the body cool. It’s rich in vitamins and essential salts.
5. Sattu Drinks
A traditional North Indian beverage made from roasted gram flour, sattu drink is high in protein and keeps you full, energized, and hydrated for longer durations.
Disclaimer:
This article is for general informational purposes only. Individual hydration needs may vary depending on age, health conditions, and activity levels. It is advisable to consult a healthcare professional or nutritionist for personalised dietary guidance, especially during extreme weather conditions.
